title: EvidenceKM: Survival Analysis of Statistical Significance Across Trust Thresholds in Cochrane Meta-Analyses
body: At what trust threshold does the statistical significance of Cochrane meta-analyses fail, and which quality components predict survival? We modelled 888 significant meta-analyses from 501 Cochrane reviews as a survival process where trust threshold acts as the time axis and loss of significance as the event. Kaplan-Meier estimation with Greenwood variance and log-log confidence intervals tracked the proportion of findings surviving at each threshold, while simplified Cox proportional hazards identified protective components. Median survival threshold was 65, meaning half of all significant findings erode below a trust score of 65, with 31.8% weakened by trust-adjusted z-statistics falling below 1.96. The log-rank test confirmed significant domain-level variation (chi-squared 19.99, four degrees of freedom, p equals 0.0005), while Cox analysis identified robustness (HR 0.161) and stability (HR 0.167) as the strongest protective factors. Survival analysis reveals a critical vulnerability zone between trust scores 60 and 75 where most significant findings collapse. These results depend on a simplified Cox approximation rather than full partial likelihood estimation.

sentences.text: At what trust threshold does the statistical significance of Cochrane meta-analyses fail, and which quality components predict survival?
We modelled 888 significant meta-analyses from 501 Cochrane reviews as a survival process where trust threshold acts as the time axis and loss of significance as the event.

Kaplan-Meier estimation with Greenwood variance and log-log confidence intervals tracked the proportion of findings surviving at each threshold, while simplified Cox proportional hazards identified protective components.
Median survival threshold was 65, meaning half of all significant findings erode below a trust score of 65, with 31.8% weakened by trust-adjusted z-statistics falling below 1.96.

The log-rank test confirmed significant domain-level variation (chi-squared 19.99, four degrees of freedom, p equals 0.0005), while Cox analysis identified robustness (HR 0.161) and stability (HR 0.167) as the strongest protective factors.
Survival analysis reveals a critical vulnerability zone between trust scores 60 and 75 where most significant findings collapse.
These results depend on a simplified Cox approximation rather than full partial likelihood estimation.
